source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed., 2021) — McChesney/Covey/Huling/Thele/Walker
_comment: Multi-mode triage gate. Tests scope detection (solo vs team-leader vs ambiguous-needs-disambiguation) + non-activation lures (already-committed / member-misfire / non-4DX / specific-discipline). Activation judgment based on description-only.

test_cases.prompt: Should I use 4DX for my marathon training goal? I want to run sub-3:30 by next October.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill; scope detection identifies solo (first-person singular + personal goal); load protocols/personal-mode.md; run 6-step Socratic triage; likely verdict: APPLICABLE (sustained behavioral change required: weekly mileage, pace work, recovery — not stroke-of-the-pen, not whirlwind).
test_cases.notes: Direct EN match: 'Should I use 4DX for X?' canonical phrasing in description; 'I' anchor → solo; no team context.

test_cases.prompt: 私の博士論文に 4DX 使えますか？3年で書き上げたいんですが、今のままだと無理そうで。
test_cases.expected_behavior: 発動; scope detection 識別 solo; load protocols/personal-mode.md; 6 段階の Socratic triage を走らせる。推定 verdict: 微妙 — 創作的アウトプット (creative-output) anti-pattern に当たる可能性あり; Goodhart 効果のリスクを説明しつつ deliberate practice + 制約ベース手法へリダイレクトの可能性も示唆。
test_cases.notes: JP match: 「この目標に 4DX 使える？」 phrasing in description; thesis writing is canonical creative-output anti-pattern.

test_cases.prompt: Should our 8-person team adopt 4DX? I'm the team lead and I keep hearing about it but I'm not sure it fits us.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill; scope detection identifies team-leader (first-person plural + 'team lead' + 'our team' + size 8); load protocols/team-mode.md; run 9-step gate ending in TEAM-APPLICABLE / one of the redirect verdicts.
test_cases.notes: Direct EN match: 'Should our team adopt 4DX?' canonical leader-asks-fit phrasing; team size in 3-12 band; BEFORE any D1 work.

test_cases.prompt: 我們團隊適合導入 4DX 嗎？10 個人，主管想推但我怕水土不服。
test_cases.expected_behavior: 啟動 skill; scope detection 識別 team-leader; load protocols/team-mode.md; 執行 9 步 Socratic 流程; 預期通過大部分 gate (size 10 在 3-12)，但 Schein assumption-conflict / Galbraith reward-system 等 readiness 檢查可能觸發 TEAM-NOT-YET-READY。
test_cases.notes: ZH match: 「我們團隊適合導入 4DX 嗎？」 canonical zh-TW phrasing in description.

test_cases.prompt: Is 4DX a good fit for what I'm trying to do? I'm not sure if it'll work.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill; scope detection finds ambiguous (no clear actor — no 'team' or 'I personally' anchor); ask ONE Socratic disambiguation question covering solo vs team-leader; route based on user's reply.
test_cases.notes: Ambiguous-scope fallback: triggers the orchestrator's disambiguation question (multilingual EN/JP/zh-TW). Tests that scope detection correctly identifies ambiguity rather than collapsing one way.

test_cases.prompt: Triage 4DX for myself — I want to lose 20 pounds in 6 months.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill, but scope is explicit ('for myself' + first-person singular + personal goal) — skip the disambiguation question; load protocols/personal-mode.md directly. Triage verdict likely APPLICABLE or habit-formation depending on Step 4 anti-pattern screen on weight-loss daily actions. Marked should_not_trigger relative to the disambiguation question (NOT relative to skill activation).
test_cases.notes: Boundary: explicit-scope-misfire test. The skill DOES activate; what should NOT trigger is the orchestrator's disambiguation prompt — scope is already clear, fire personal-mode directly. Tests SKILL.md's 'If signal is strong, skip the question' clause.

test_cases.prompt: I've decided to use 4DX for my Q3 OKR — 'launch v2 of the product by Sept 30'. I have my WIG. What lead measures should I pick? I'm thinking weekly user-interview count and PR-merge cadence.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. User has already committed to 4DX AND has a defined WIG; they're asking a D2-specific (lead measure discovery) question. Per description's non-activation: 'NOT for already-committed users asking how to start (route to D1)' → route to 4dx-d2-lead-measures.
test_cases.notes: Lure: already-triaged / D2-question. Description explicitly excludes this case.

test_cases.prompt: I just joined a team that already uses 4DX. My manager already picked the WIG. Can you explain how the WIG and lead measures work so I understand the cadence?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill — OR activate and immediately route out via SKILL.md's member-edge-case clause. User is a member receiving an inherited WIG; members don't triage methodology fit. Route to 4dx-d1-wig-formulation.
test_cases.notes: Lure: member-misfire — description explicitly states 'NOT for team members inheriting a WIG (route to 4dx-d1-wig-formulation — members don't triage methodology fit)'. SKILL.md has explicit member edge-case routing.

test_cases.prompt: Help me set up OKRs for my team next quarter — we have 3 main objectives and need to define key results for each.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. Non-4DX methodology query (OKR). Per description: 'NOT for ... non-4DX methodologies (OKR / agile / habit-stacking / scrum / kanban) → hand off via using-four-dx-coach'.
test_cases.notes: Lure: non-4DX methodology — should hand off to plugin router, not run triage.

test_cases.prompt: 我們公司 50 個 team 要導入 4DX，下個季度開始。請幫我設計 cascade WIG 的部署計畫，包含 leader-of-leaders training schedule。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. Enterprise multi-team rollout. Per description: 'NOT for enterprise multi-team rollout (read book Ch 6-10 directly + 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade)'. Single-team scope only.
test_cases.notes: Lure: enterprise-rollout — actor model (50 teams + leader-of-leaders) differs from single-team triage scope.

test_cases.prompt: I lead an 8-person ER (emergency room) trauma team. My boss wants us to adopt 4DX. Should we?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill; scope detection → team-leader; load protocols/team-mode.md; pass Steps 1-3, but Step 4 firefighting-role check returns wrong-team-shape (firefighting role) verdict — 4DX inverts wrong even at team-size sweet spot when team's mission IS the whirlwind.
test_cases.notes: Boundary: team size in 3-12 band but role is structurally firefighting — the wrong-team-shape (firefighting) verdict path catches this, not just size-out-of-band cases.

test_cases.prompt: I want to read more this year. Should I use 4DX?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate skill; scope = solo; load protocols/personal-mode.md. Step 1 (Surface goal in concrete terms) forces disambiguation — could be habit-formation ('read 10 min/night') OR behavioral-change WIG ('From 4 books/yr to 24 books/yr by Dec 31'). Routes via Step 4 habit screen vs Step 7 APPLICABLE based on user's clarification.
test_cases.notes: Boundary: ambiguous goal scale within solo mode. Tests that Step 1 (concrete-terms forcing) correctly handles habit-vs-WIG ambiguity rather than collapsing one way.

ship_threshold_note: 100% to ship cleanly. ≥80% allowed only with explicit failure analysis. <80% must redo Stage 2 A2/E/B. should_not_trigger has zero tolerance.

strengths: Multilingual triggers per scope (solo + team-leader + ambiguous-fallback) explicitly listed in description.
Non-activation clauses cover all four documented lure types: already-committed → D1 routing; member → comprehension routing; non-4DX → plugin router; enterprise → book chapters.
Member-edge-case has a dedicated SKILL.md clause that activates the skill briefly then routes out — handles T-08 cleanly without bypassing the description gate.
Verdict enumeration in description tells the agent the OUTPUT shape per mode, reducing ambiguity about whether a habit / firefighting / portfolio query is in-scope.
minor_observations: T-06 (explicit-scope) tests an interesting case: skill fires but skips the disambiguation question. SKILL.md handles via the 'If signal is strong, skip the question' clause. No fix needed.
T-12 (ambiguous goal scale) is parallel to T-06 — scope is clear (solo) but goal-scale within solo is ambiguous; protocol's Step 1 handles via concrete-terms forcing. No fix needed.
